description_long: This object is a heavy, dual-barreled rotary cannon system, likely designed for mounting on a combat tactical vehicle. The main body is robust and cylindrical, with two long barrels extending forward, encased in a reinforced housing. The rear section features a circular shroud and mechanical components, including a visible motor or drive unit mounted to the side. The structure is predominantly metallic, with a dark olive-green and gray finish, and displays exposed mechanical linkages and mounting brackets. The overall design suggests a focus on durability and high-rate-of-fire capability, with no visible crew compartment or wheels. The asset is compact but dense, with no visible armor plating or camouflage pattern.
description: A heavy, dual-barreled rotary cannon system with a robust metallic body, side-mounted drive unit, and exposed mechanical components. Finished in dark olive-green and gray, it is designed for mounting on combat vehicles.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular shroud, twin barrels, and reinforced housing.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the barrels, side-mounted drive unit, and mounting brackets.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the twin barrels' alignment and the mechanical linkage at the back.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the cylindrical body, drive unit, and mounting structure.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One visible rotary cannon mount with dual barrels, located at the front of the system; side mounting brackets for vehicle attachment.
attribute_armor_plating: None visible; the structure appears to be unarmored with exposed mechanical components.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; the finish is a solid dark olive-green and gray.
